title: salix drumensis
intro: Salix drummondiana, also known as Drummond's willow, is a species of willow tree native to western North America. This deciduous tree has medicinal properties due to its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Salix drummondiana include its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, making it useful for treating headaches, fever, and rheumatism. Salix drummondiana can be prepared as a tea or tincture for internal use, or as a salve for topical application.
